summary: Product manager with experience building consumer and fintech products. Passionate about solving user problems and working with cross-functional teams.

bullets.text: Owned the merchant onboarding product line for Cashfree's payment gateway
Led a cross-functional team of 6 (engineering, design, data) to launch a revamped KYC flow, reducing merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours
Responsible for the roadmap of the risk and fraud detection product area
Worked closely with compliance and legal teams on RBI regulatory requirements
Launched a self-serve merchant dashboard that increased weekly active merchants by 22%
Partnered with data science to introduce a fraud scoring model, cutting chargeback rate by 31%
Managed the restaurant partner rating and feedback product
Drove A/B experiments on the delivery partner app, improving order acceptance rate by 9%
Coordinated with 4 engineering pods across two cities to ship a partner payout redesign
Responsible for writing PRDs and prioritizing the backlog for the partner tools squad
Worked on the search and discovery team
Assisted senior PMs with user research and competitive analysis
Contributed to a redesign of the restaurant listing page

projects.name: Campus Meal-Split App (side project)
projects.description: Built a simple expense-splitting app for hostel meal groups using React Native and Firebase
metrics: Used by around 300 students at IIT Delhi during a 6-month pilot

achievements.description: Reduced merchant onboarding time by leading redesign of the KYC flow
Increased weekly active merchants via a new self-serve merchant dashboard
Cut chargeback rate by introducing a fraud scoring model with data science
Improved delivery partner app order acceptance rate via A/B experiments

leadership_experience: Led a cross-functional team of 6 (engineering, design, data) to launch a revamped KYC flow (Cashfree)
Coordinated across 4 engineering pods in two cities to ship a partner payout redesign (Swiggy) — coordination, not direct people management
product_experience: Owned merchant onboarding and risk/fraud detection roadmap at Cashfree Payments (fintech/payments)
Managed restaurant partner rating/feedback product and partner tools backlog at Swiggy (marketplace)
Contributed to search & discovery and restaurant listing page redesign at Zomato as Associate PM (marketplace)
technical_experience: Uses SQL for product analytics (stated skill, not tied to a specific bullet)
Built a side-project mobile app (Campus Meal-Split) using React Native and Firebase, demonstrating hands-on technical fluency uncommon for a PM

metrics.metric: Merchant onboarding time reduced from 3 days to 18 hours

metrics.context: A/B experiments on delivery partner app

strongest_achievements: Reduced merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours by leading a KYC flow redesign (Cashfree)
Cut chargeback rate by 31% by introducing a fraud scoring model (Cashfree)
Increased weekly active merchants by 22% via a self-serve dashboard launch (Cashfree)
weakest_sections: Zomato entry: all three bullets ('Worked on...', 'Assisted...', 'Contributed to...') are generic with no scope, scale, or outcome
Summary section is generic boilerplate that does not differentiate the candidate or preview the strongest achievements below it
Certifications section states '(none listed)' rather than being omitted, drawing attention to an empty section
missing_metrics: 'Owned the merchant onboarding product line' (Cashfree) — no scale given (e.g. merchant volume, transaction value)
'Responsible for the roadmap of the risk and fraud detection product area' (Cashfree) — no outcome or scale stated
'Helped improve partner satisfaction scores' (Swiggy) — no baseline, target, or resulting number
'Coordinated...to ship a partner payout redesign' (Swiggy) — ships the project but states no resulting impact
'Managed the restaurant partner rating and feedback product' (Swiggy) — no scale (# partners) or outcome (rating change)
All three Zomato bullets lack any metric, scope, or outcome
redundant_content: 'Responsible for' is used as a bullet opener twice (Cashfree risk/fraud roadmap; Swiggy PRDs/backlog), diluting both
Summary section restates the fintech/consumer domain claim without adding information beyond what the work experience already shows

weak_action_verbs.issue: 'Responsible for' is a passive, ownership-diluting opener
weak_action_verbs.location: Cashfree Payments — risk and fraud roadmap bullet
weak_action_verbs.recommendation: Replace with an active verb naming the outcome, e.g. 'Drove the risk and fraud detection roadmap, shipping X that reduced Y.'

weak_action_verbs.issue: 'Responsible for' repeated as a weak opener
weak_action_verbs.recommendation: Replace with 'Authored PRDs and prioritized the backlog for...' and state a resulting outcome.

weak_action_verbs.issue: 'Helped improve' is vague and undersells individual ownership
weak_action_verbs.recommendation: State the specific action taken and the resulting score change, e.g. 'Raised partner satisfaction score from X to Y by...'

weak_action_verbs.issue: 'Worked on' is a low-signal opener that implies no ownership or contribution
weak_action_verbs.recommendation: Name the specific contribution made and its result.

weak_action_verbs.issue: 'Assisted' and 'Contributed to' understate scope for the entire Zomato entry
weak_action_verbs.recommendation: If the associate-level scope is accurate, keep the framing but name the specific research findings or redesign elements owned.

ats_formatting_issues.issue: Em dash ('—') used to separate title, company, and location
ats_formatting_issues.recommendation: Use a comma or pipe ('|') separator, which parses more reliably across ATS systems.

ats_formatting_issues.issue: En dash ('–') used between start and end dates
ats_formatting_issues.location: All work experience and education date lines
ats_formatting_issues.recommendation: Use a plain hyphen ('-') for maximum ATS date-parsing compatibility.

ats_formatting_issues.issue: Certifications section contains literal placeholder text '(none listed)'
ats_formatting_issues.recommendation: Omit the section entirely rather than including an explicit empty placeholder.

ats_formatting_issues.issue: Skills are listed as a single comma-separated paragraph rather than grouped or bulleted
ats_formatting_issues.recommendation: Group into categories (e.g. Product, Technical, Tools) for better keyword scannability by both ATS and recruiters.

human_readable_summary:
## Resume Analysis: Ananya Sharma — Senior Product Manager

**Strongest signal:** Three well-quantified, high-impact achievements from the current Cashfree role — a 3-day-to-18-hour onboarding time cut, a 31% chargeback reduction, and a 22% lift in weekly active merchants. These are the resume's headline material and should lead any rewrite.

**Main concern:** Roughly half the bullets across all three roles (especially the entire Zomato entry) use vague, low-ownership framing ('Worked on', 'Assisted', 'Responsible for', 'Helped improve') with no scope or outcome attached. This creates an uneven resume where strong recent work is undercut by generic earlier entries.

**Career trajectory:** Consistent upward movement — Associate PM (Zomato) → PM (Swiggy) → Senior PM (Cashfree) — across marketplace and fintech domains, roughly 8 years of experience assuming 'Present' resolves to the analysis date.

**Quick wins:** Add scale/outcome numbers to the ~6 identified metric-less bullets, remove the repeated 'Responsible for' framing, and drop the empty Certifications section rather than flagging it as blank.
limitations: Duration for the current Cashfree role and total years of experience assume 'Present' resolves to the analysis date (2026-07-10); actual tenure will differ if this resume is reviewed later without updating.
Several bullets lack scope indicators (team size, user base, budget), so seniority/scope cannot be fully corroborated beyond what's explicitly stated.
No portfolio links, publications, or awards exist beyond what's listed, so nothing beyond the stated content could be verified.
